mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate above a 0.5 percentage point quarterly threshold signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient at p less than 0.05 in a multivariate regression over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent annualized, further increases in the federal funds rate produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to growth fears outweighing valuation support.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate during high inflation rate hikes, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth rates over a 10-year out-of-sample period, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production drops below the 95.0 threshold, it signals a severe supply-side contraction that forces equity repricing, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold breach fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 50 percent in a walk-forward test across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between payroll changes and unemployment changes yields an insignificant coefficient in predictive models over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity trap regime where the federal funds rate is near zero, declines in retail sales produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as monetary policy loses transmission power.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent, or the signal reverses direction in normal rate environments.
